comments.text: This problem should have an extra condition that $(a_n)$ is strictly increasing. I find it implicitly understood in [ErGr80,p.64] that this condition carries over from the previous page. In a later paper dedicated to his late colleague (p. 334) Erdős begins the paragraph with $n_1\leqq\cdots$, suggesting that the sequence was assumed to be at least non-decreasing. In [KoTa24] we assumed that $a_1<a_2<a_3<\cdots$ was understood in all questions of this type. Another argument supporting the monotonicity condition: without it the remaining problem on whether it is possible to achieve $\limsup a_n^{1/2^n}>1$ would be entirely trivial. Just take any sequence making both $\sum_{n}1/a_n$ and $\sum_{n}1/(a_n-1)$ rational and rearrange it to have an extremely rapidly growing subsequence. This whole remark might be obvious and redundant. I agree that it only makes sense to take $a_n$ in the increasing order, but it wouldn't hurt to be precise, to avoid trivial "solutions" to the problem or its subproblems. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
The link to "later paper" doesn't seem to work anymore. I believe this is a working link: https://www.renyi.hu/~p_erdos/1985-10.pdf.
